specific_location: On Greenhorn Creek just west of the Meadow Creek Lane cul-de-sac
description: I did not see this bird well so the ID is solely based on its song described above. It was very close in size to nearby Evening Grosbeaks and had a thinner bill than the Grosbeaks.Behaviors: The bird sang atop a tall cottonwood and only moved when flying away as a Cooper's Hawk flew by.Call: A flute like "ee-o-lay" sometimes preceded by a low soft husky "tuk-tuk-tuk." Variant songs were "ee-o-lay" immediately followed by a rising "ee-yeeyeeyee" or a lower "yer" note.Plumage: Assumed adult
